technologies.name: Torch
technologies.description:
Torch is an open-source deep learning framework that was originally developed in the Lua programming language. It provided a powerful interface for building neural networks and performing scientific computing.

Torch has been largely replaced by PyTorch, and it is now considered outdated for most modern deep learning tasks.

technologies.name: PyTorch

technologies.name: Joda-Time
technologies.description:
Joda-Time provides a quality replacement for the Java date and time classes.

Joda-Time is the de facto standard date and time library for Java prior to Java SE 8. Users are now asked to migrate to java.time (JSR-310).

Joda-time is no longer in active development except to keep timezone data up to date.

technologies.name: Debezium
technologies.description:
Debezium is an open-source Change Data Capture (CDC) platform that captures database changes in real-time and streams them to applications using Apache Kafka. It allows event-driven architectures to react to data changes without polling the database.
Category: Data Streaming / Change Data Capture (CDC)
Main Use Case: Capturing inserts, updates, and deletes from databases and propagating them to Kafka topics or other event-driven systems.

technologies.name: Apache Phoenix
technologies.description: Apache Phoenix is an open source, massively parallel, relational database engine supporting OLTP for Hadoop using Apache HBase as its backing store. Phoenix provides a JDBC driver that hides the intricacies of the NoSQL store enabling users to create, delete, and alter SQL tables, views, indexes, and sequences; insert and delete rows singly and in bulk; and query data through SQL.[1] Phoenix compiles queries and other statements into native NoSQL store APIs rather than using MapReduce enabling the building of low latency applications on top of NoSQL stores

technologies.name: WSO2
technologies.description:
Foundational technology for application development and identity & access management
Digital experiences delivered as apps, workflows, and automations require powerful cloud native applications to do the heavy lifting. Our application development and IAM technologies, available as open source or SaaS, take away most of the complexity and let your developers focus only on your business requirements.

technologies.name: WSO2 Carbon
technologies.description: WSO2 Carbon is the core platform on which WSO2 middleware products are built. It is based on Java OSGi technology, which allows components to be dynamically installed, started, stopped, updated, and uninstalled, and it eliminates component version conflicts. In Carbon, this capability translates into a solid core of common middleware enterprise components, including clustering, security, logging, and monitoring, plus the ability to add components for specific features needed to solve a specific enterprise scenario.

technologies.name: Checker Framework
technologies.description:
The Checker Framework is a static analysis tool that enhances Java's type system by adding additional checks to detect and prevent errors at compile time. It helps catch null pointer exceptions, incorrect units, security vulnerabilities, and more before running the program.

🔹 Key Responsibilities:

Enhances Java's type system (e.g., nullness checking, immutability, security).

Detects programming errors at compile time.

Integrates with Java compilers and build tools (Maven, Gradle, IntelliJ, Eclipse).

Used in security-sensitive applications to enforce stricter type rules.
